originalTitle: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DR Congo an international emergency
title: Friends Helping Friends in Congo Stay Healthy!
content:
Hello from Your Kid Reporter!

Hi everyone! Your super kid reporter from Africa is here! Today, we have some important news. It's all about helping our friends in a country called DR Congo stay safe and healthy. DR Congo is a very big and beautiful country right here in our amazing continent, Africa! Sometimes, news can sound a bit grown-up, but I'm going to tell you all about it in simple words, just for you!

What is DR Congo?

DR Congo is short for the Democratic Republic of Congo. It's one of the biggest countries in Africa. It has big rivers, tall trees, and lots of different animals. Many children just like you live there. Did you know? Africa is a huge continent with many different countries, languages, and cultures! We are all part of one big African family.

What is Ebola?

Okay, so there's a tiny, tiny germ that can sometimes make people very, very sick. This germ is called Ebola. It's like a very, very strong flu or cold. When someone gets Ebola, they can feel very tired, have a fever (that means they feel very hot), tummy aches, and sometimes feel like throwing up. It's a sickness that needs a lot of careful help from doctors and nurses. Did you know? Germs are tiny living things that we can't see without a special microscope. Some germs are good, but some can make us sick!

Who is WHO?

Now, you might hear about something called the 'WHO'. This is super important! WHO stands for the World Health Organization. You can think of them as a very, very big team of doctors and health helpers for the whole world! They have smart people who work very hard to keep everyone healthy. When a sickness like Ebola pops up, WHO helps countries know what to do and sends helpers. Did you know? The WHO has offices all over the world, including many in Africa, to help people in different countries.

What is an 'International Emergency'?

The WHO recently said that the Ebola sickness in DR Congo is an 'international emergency'. That sounds a bit scary, right? But don't worry! It just means that lots and lots of people from all over the world need to come together and help. It's like when there's a big problem, and everyone helps to fix it. This is a call for extra help! It means that doctors, nurses, and special health workers from different countries will go to DR Congo to lend a hand. They will help the doctors and nurses already there to fight the sickness. Did you know? When many countries work together, it's called 'international cooperation'. It's like a big team working to solve a puzzle!

How Does Ebola Spread (and How to Stay Safe)?

This is very important to understand. Ebola does *not* spread easily through the air like a normal cold or cough. You can't catch it just by standing next to someone. It spreads if you touch someone who is very, very sick, or their blood, or their body fluids. It can also spread if you touch things that a very sick person has used. That is why doctors and nurses wear special suits and gloves when they are helping sick people. These special clothes protect them! Did you know? Washing your hands with soap and clean water is one of the best ways to get rid of germs and stay healthy! You should wash your hands often, especially before eating and after using the toilet.

Many People Are Helping!

The news told us that some people in DR Congo got sick. Around 246 people got this sickness. Sadly, some people didn't get better, about 80 of them. This makes everyone very sad. But remember, many, many kind and brave people are working super hard to help. They are giving medicine, taking care of the sick, and teaching everyone how to stay safe. They are like superheroes, helping our friends in DR Congo get better and stop the sickness from spreading to more people. Did you know? Many local people in DR Congo, like community leaders and volunteers, are also working very hard to educate their neighbors and friends about how to stay safe.

It's Not a 'Pandemic'!

Here's some really good news! The WHO also said that this sickness is *not* a 'pandemic'. What's a pandemic? A pandemic is when a sickness is *everywhere* in the whole wide world, making lots and lots of people sick in many, many countries. That's a very big sickness! The Ebola sickness in DR Congo is mostly in one part of that country, and the helpers are working hard to keep it there. So, it is *not* everywhere. This is a big relief! Did you know? Diseases can be classified differently based on how they spread. A 'local outbreak' is just in one place, while a 'pandemic' is global.

What Can We Do?

Even if we live far away, we can all do our part. We can learn about staying clean and healthy. This helps us and our families stay safe from all kinds of germs. Always wash your hands with soap and water for as long as it takes to sing 'Happy Birthday' twice! Tell a grown-up if you don't feel well. We can also send good thoughts to our friends in DR Congo. We hope they get all the help they need and that everyone gets well very, very soon. Did you know? Sharing kindness and hope can make a big difference, even from far away!

Stay Healthy and Happy!

So, remember, there's a sickness in DR Congo, but many kind and brave people are working together to help. It's not a global sickness, and our friends are getting lots of support. Keep washing those hands, listen to your grown-ups, and stay healthy! We are all one big African family, caring for each other. That's all for today from your kid reporter. Stay curious and stay healthy!
summary: Kind helpers from all over the world are working hard in DR Congo, a country in Africa, to help people stay healthy from a sickness called Ebola, and many people are getting the help they need.
whyMatters: This is important because it teaches us how brave people help others who are sick, how to stay healthy by washing our hands, and that we should care about our friends in other parts of Africa.

originalTitle: Toddlers among more than 50 schoolchildren kidnapped in Nigeria
title: Hope for Our School Friends in Nigeria
content:
Hello, little explorers and brave hearts! Today, we have some news from a beautiful country in Africa called Nigeria. It's a bit of sad news, but it's important to talk about it. We want to share what happened and remind everyone that many good people are working hard to help.

A Sad Day in Mussa Town

In a small town called Mussa, in a part of Nigeria called Borno state, something very sad happened. One day, more than 50 school children were taken away from their homes and school. These children were of different ages. Some were even very, very tiny, like toddlers who are just learning to walk and talk. Imagine your little brother or sister, or a friend who is just starting to go to school – some of them were that small. This happened just recently, and it made many people in Mussa town very, very sad and worried. Everyone wants these children to come back home safely to their families and friends. Their toys miss them, their beds miss them, and most of all, their families miss them very much.

What Does Kidnapped Mean?

When we say children were 'kidnapped,' it means that some people took them away without their parents' permission. It means they were taken when they should have been safe at home or at school. This is a very bad thing to do. Grown-ups everywhere know it is wrong to take children away from their families. It is not fair, and it makes everyone very worried. No special group has said, “We did this.” So, we don't know exactly who took them. But what we do know is that many people are looking for the children and want them to be found. Everyone wants them to be safe and sound back with their loved ones. Did you know? In many parts of the world, there are rules and laws to protect children. These rules say that children must always be safe and loved by their families. Taking a child is against these important rules.

Where is Mussa Town?

Mussa town is a quiet place in Nigeria. Nigeria is a very big country in West Africa. It has many, many people and lots of different languages and cultures. Borno State is a part of Nigeria, like how your country might have different states or provinces. Imagine a map of Africa. Nigeria is on the western side, touching the big blue ocean. Borno State is in the northeastern part of Nigeria, far away from the ocean. It's a place with lots of open land and small villages, where children often play outside and go to local schools. Did you know? Africa is the second-largest continent in the world! It has 54 different countries, and each one is special in its own way. Nigeria is one of the biggest countries in Africa.

Who Are the Children?

The children who were taken are school kids, just like you! Some of them were toddlers, which means they are very, very young, maybe only 2 or 3 years old. Others were a bit older, maybe 6, 7, or 8 years old, who love to learn and play with their friends. These children love to play games, tell stories, and learn new things in school. They have dreams, just like you do. They want to grow up and be doctors, teachers, farmers, or anything they can imagine. Their families miss their happy voices and their laughter.

How Do People Feel?

When something like this happens, it makes many people feel very sad and scared. The parents and families of the children are heartbroken. They miss their little ones so much and are very worried about them. Their friends at school miss them too. The teachers miss seeing their happy faces in the classroom. People in Mussa town and all over Nigeria are feeling this sadness. They are thinking about the children and their families every day. When someone you love goes missing, it feels like a piece of your heart is missing too.

What Are People Doing to Help?

Even though it's a very sad time, many people are working hard to help. The police and the army in Nigeria are looking everywhere for the children. They are searching in towns and in the countryside. They want to find them and bring them home safely. People in Mussa town and other places are also helping each other. They are praying for the children. They are supporting the families and hoping for good news. When a community comes together, they are stronger. Everyone wants to see these children back in their beds and back in their schools. Did you know? When people help each other in times of trouble, it's called 'community spirit.' It means everyone cares and works together to make things better.

Why is School So Important?

School is a very special place. It’s where you learn to read, write, and count. It's where you make friends and discover new things about the world. Going to school helps you grow smart and strong, and it helps you imagine a wonderful future. When children are taken from school, it stops them from learning and playing. This is why it's so important that all children get to go to school and be safe there. Every child deserves to learn and play and dream big dreams. Did you know? Learning helps you understand the world around you! The more you learn, the more exciting your life can be!

A Message of Hope

We know this news is sad, but we want you to remember something very important: grown-ups all over the world, and especially in Nigeria, are working very hard to keep children safe. They are trying their best to find these children and bring them home. We must never give up hope. We believe that these children will be found, and they will be able to go back to their schools, their families, and their happy lives. Let’s all send our kind thoughts to the children from Mussa town and their families. We hope they come home very, very soon!
summary: More than 50 school children, including toddlers, were sadly taken from their homes and school in Mussa town, Nigeria, and many people are working hard to find them and bring them back safely.
whyMatters: This is important because every child deserves to be safe, go to school, and be with their family, and when this doesn't happen, it makes everyone sad and worried, so we hope they come home soon.

title: Senior IS leader killed in joint operation, US and Nigeria say
content: President Donald Trump said the operation eliminated "the most active terrorist in the world", Abu-Bilal al-Minuki.

title: How worrying is the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo?

originalTitle: Title decider not cut short by pitch invasion - SPFL
title: Title decider not cut short by pitch invasion - SPFL
content: The SPFL confirms Celtic's title-clinching victory over Hearts did not end prematurely, while condemning the pitch invasion after Celtic's third goal.
